opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — it's her mum. She's screaming, clutching her chest and saying her heart is going to explode ever since the fire truck went past with its siren about two hours ago. She's breathing really fast, she's gone pale and she nearly passed out. We can't calm her down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A loud siren set her off two hours ago and she has not settled — she keeps saying her chest hurts and her heart is racing, and she went wobbly like she might faint.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, right after a fire truck went past the house with its siren on
symptom_narrative.character: screaming and crying, clutching the middle of her chest, saying her heart is going to explode, breathing very fast and deep
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we have ever seen her — she cannot be calmed down at all
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since the siren, not letting up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — we tried talking to her and it hasn't helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: loud noises set it off and any noise seems to make it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing heart that won't slow down, chest pain, dizziness and nearly fainting
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot be consoled, cannot talk to us properly, cannot do anything

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Williams syndrome, diagnosed as a young child
past_medical_history.how_managed: followed up by paediatric and genetics teams; usually very sociable and chatty

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a narrowing of the main valve/artery coming out of her heart that the heart specialists keep an eye on
past_medical_history.how_managed: monitored by cardiology; not on any heart medicines

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: very sensitive to loud noises — sudden loud sounds really distress her

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 14 years old, lives with parents, attends mainstream school with support; the house is on a busy road so sirens and traffic noise are common

objective_data_offered.value: her smartwatch is showing a heart rate of about 145 and it won't come down
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single wearable reading taken during extreme distress and movement — may not be accurate

objective_data_offered.value: she looks pale, is breathing very fast and deep, is clutching the centre of her chest and crying
she went wobbly and said everything went dizzy, like she was about to faint
the home is on a busy road; her noise-cancelling headphones are here but she wasn't wearing them when the siren went past

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: Williams syndrome — developmental and intellectual effects; normally very sociable and verbal but currently unable to communicate because of extreme distress
